methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $1.5B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 5 same-sector + same-stage peers in Defense & Security. Range: $1.0B - $1.5B.

data.narrative:
Vannevar Labs, a Series B defense and security company founded by Brett Granberg and Nini Hamrick, is evaluated against a universe of 10 comparable companies—2 public (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private firms in the same sector and stage. The target carries a current post-money valuation of $1.5B following $91M in total capital raised.

Two triangulation methods apply to this assessment. Stage-progression analysis applies a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical of Series B advancement to the $1.5B base, yielding $2.9B. Peer-median analysis examines five same-sector, same-stage comparables—Dream Security ($1.5B), Harmattan AI ($1.4B), Castelion ($1.0B), Defense Unicorns ($1.0B), and Heven AeroTech ($1.0B)—producing a median valuation of $1.0B with a range spanning $1.0B to $1.5B. The disparity between these methods reflects uncertainty in stage-progression assumptions versus observable market pricing.

Triangulation across both methods supports a valuation range of $850M (low) to $3.3B (high), with a median estimate of $1.9B. The current $1.5B valuation sits below the median but within the peer range, suggesting pricing at the upper quartile of comparable Series B transactions. Key uncertainties include capital efficiency relative to peer cohort, competitive positioning within defense technology, and the appropriate stage-advancement multiple given market conditions.
